author: mark
text:
State worktree lock for concurrent bl writes

Parallelism blocker fixed. commit_task, commit_staged, and
remove_task now hold a store-wide flock at
.balls/local/lock/state-worktree.lock so git's index.lock
never sees contention. Empirically verified: without the
lock, 6 of 8 parallel bl create workers fail with 'fatal:
Unable to create index.lock'; with the lock, all 8 succeed
across 5 consecutive test runs.

The old archive_task + commit_staged pair was a two-step
sequence that was also race-prone across the gap. Replaced
with a single close_and_archive(task, msg) method that runs
parent-bookkeeping + git rm + git commit under one lock.
Reviewer messages ride in the commit body now, which also
fixes a latent bug where append_note_to was writing to a
notes file that got immediately git-rm'd.

New tests/concurrent_writes.rs stresses the path with 8
threads doing a mix of create + update.
